title: Get the Size of a DataFrame

testcases.input: +-----------+----------+-----+-------------+--------------------+ | player_id | name | age | position | team | +-----------+----------+-----+-------------+--------------------+ | 846 | Mason | 21 | Forward | RealMadrid | | 749 | Riley | 30 | Winger | Barcelona | | 155 | Bob | 28 | Striker | ManchesterUnited | | 583 | Isabella | 32 | Goalkeeper | Liverpool | | 388 | Zachary | 24 | Midfielder | BayernMunich | | 883 | Ava | 23 | Defender | Chelsea | | 355 | Violet | 18 | Striker | Juventus | | 247 | Thomas | 27 | Striker | ParisSaint-Germain | | 761 | Jack | 33 | Midfielder | ManchesterCity | | 642 | Charlie | 36 | Center-back | Arsenal | +-----------+----------+-----+-------------+--------------------+ Output: [10, 5] Explanation: This DataFrame contains 10 rows and 5 columns.
testcases.expectedOutput: [10, 5] Explanation: This DataFrame contains 10 rows and 5 columns.
